description: Current actionable recommendations. Generated each session based on signals, predictions, and market forecasts. Accuracy is tracked to build confidence over time.

market_regime.label: BLOCKADE_ESCALATION_DIPLOMACY_ACCELERATING
market_regime.dominant_risk: US naval blockade ACTIVE but diplomacy accelerating. Second round Iran-US talks expected today/tomorrow (April 16-17). April 21-22 ceasefire expiry = 5-6 days. Trump says war 'close to over'. IRGC 'last warning' not formally retracted.
market_regime.key_risk: IRGC autonomous action — any Hormuz incident (mine, seizure, missile) overrides diplomatic progress completely. April 22 deadline is the next binary event.
market_regime.crude_current_zone: DECLINING_WATCH_ZONE ($92-96 — declining from blockade peak of $102.26, -8% in 2 sessions)
market_regime.regime_note: April 15: Nifty opened at 23,772 (gap -0.29%) but rallied to close ~24,075 (+0.97% from Apr 13 close). Brent fell to ~$93-96 (from $102.26 peak = -8%). Trump says war 'close to over'. Second round Iran-US talks expected this week (possibly today April 16 = Thursday). April 22 ceasefire expiry = 5-6 days — urgency building. IREDA earnings today = PSU green energy catalyst. P021 TCS WRONG, P024 Nifty 23200-23700 WRONG — both scored today.
market_regime.next_binary_event: 2026-04-22 ceasefire expiry + April 16-17 = Second round Iran-US talks (if confirmed)

actions.instruction: WATCH — LOW urgency. Regime shifted to DIPLOMACY_ACCELERATING. SWIGGY remains depressed (~₹280 range, April 13). P001 bearish thesis (₹260) still open but further weakened. Market at 24,150 (April 16 open) = risk-on environment. Bearish conviction for P001 very low now. No stock-specific catalyst until Q4 results (May 8). If market continues rallying on Iran talks, SWIGGY likely to drift up. Keep WATCH but lower priority. Valid until April 22 binary event.
actions.rationale_short: P001 target (₹260) requires -7.7% fall from ₹281. Ceasefire headwind to bearish thesis. Sell window ₹278-285 still valid intraday.
actions.confidence_basis: Ceasefire reduces bearish conviction. ₹260 remains structural risk but timing is compressed to 16 days. Confidence maintained at 2.

signal_details.T001: ₹260 52-week support — SWIGGY needs -7.7% to breach. Ceasefire gives partial relief.
signal_details.M003: FII April 8 provisional flows positive on ceasefire. Reduces immediate selling pressure.

actions.pnl_impact_if_right: Exit at ₹278-285 protects against P001 downside to ₹260
actions.pnl_impact_if_wrong: If SWIGGY rallies to ₹295+ on market continuation, missed upside
actions.risk: April 10 talks failure = risk-off, SWIGGY could see broader market drag

actions.instruction: HOLD — Thesis strongly intact. April 16: Brent ~$93-96 (down from $102.26). ATF cost relief FULLY RESTORED below $100. IndiGo is a major beneficiary. Iran talks progressing = base case Brent holds below $96 near term. Sell trigger (Brent >$115) remains INACTIVE. DO NOT SELL. Watch: Iran second round talks today — if formal deal announced, Brent could fall to $85-90 → IndiGo +8-12%. Hard stop: only if IRGC incident causes Brent spike >$110.
actions.rationale_short: Brent at $97.50 = ATF relief returning. Hold — thesis strengthened on Iran outreach.
actions.confidence_basis: Regime de-escalation removes immediate sell trigger. Talks outcome April 10 is the next binary for INDIGO.

signal_details.M001: Brent at $93.76 — well below $115 sell trigger. ATF cost relief is real and durable.

actions.pnl_impact_if_right: Holding INDIGO to May Q4 results. P015 already scoring RIGHT.
actions.pnl_impact_if_wrong: April 10 talks fail → Brent spikes → ATF concern returns → INDIGO gives back 3-5%
actions.risk: Islamabad talks failure = Brent spike = INDIGO headwind

actions.instruction: HOLD — P005 (>₹430 by May 28) very comfortable. Coal India at ~₹449 (April 13). Buffer of ₹19 with 42 days remaining. Oil price decline has no direct negative impact on coal demand. Domestic power sector demand strong. DII buying supporting PSU floor. No action needed. Review only if closes below ₹440.
actions.rationale_short: P005 Coal India thesis at confidence 4★. ₹449.20 vs ₹430 target — very safe with 49 days remaining.
actions.confidence_basis: P005 tracking comfortably. ₹19 buffer above target. Domestic demand insulation intact.

signal_details.S008: Coal e-auction premiums elevated — power sector demand strong
signal_details.M006: PSU rerating theme — Coal India dividend yield provides price floor

actions.pnl_impact_if_right: P005 confirms >₹430 by May 28 — very likely
actions.pnl_impact_if_wrong: Broad market selloff on talks failure could drag -2% but ₹430 floor intact
actions.risk: Ceasefire reduces crude-coal differential slightly. But coal's cost advantage remains.

actions.instruction: HOLD — Upgraded on Iran outreach. April 15: Iran outreach signal positive for Hormuz transit. If US-Iran talks resume → Hormuz re-opens → ADANI PORT direct beneficiary. P019 target ₹1,490-1,540 comes back into play. Do not sell — wait for April 22 deadline. If formal talks announced → upgrade to BUY (Hormuz reopening thesis). Risk: Iran rejects outreach → Hormuz remains contested → trim 25%.
actions.rationale_short: Hormuz safe passage = direct ADANI PORT revenue catalyst. Stock at ₹1,453, target ₹1,490-1,540 in 7D.
actions.confidence_basis: P019 thesis is multi-factor. April 10 talks are the key risk. Ceasefire day 2 = holding.

signal_details.M001: Hormuz safe passage protocol confirmed — shipping lanes open
signal_details.S005: Port infrastructure re-rating on traffic restoration visibility

actions.pnl_impact_if_wrong: April 10 talks fail → Hormuz concern returns → give back gains

actions.instruction: SETFGOLD — UPGRADED to HIGH urgency. PARTIAL PROFIT TRIGGER IMMINENT. Iran second round talks expected TODAY (April 16) or Friday. P023 target ₹130-134: if SETFGOLD is at or above ₹130, this is the EXIT TRIGGER. Action: If SETFGOLD ≥ ₹130, SELL 30-40% of position TODAY. Rationale: Iran formal talks = war premium easing → gold demand as safe haven REDUCING. Keep 60-70% position as hedge against IRGC incident risk until April 22 ceasefire expiry. Do NOT hold full position into Iran deal confirmation — war premium exit = gold decline of 3-5%. Entry was ~₹120 (estimated). Current ~₹127.6 (April 13). Check current price vs ₹130 trigger.
actions.rationale_short: War premium easing but alive at Brent $97.50. Hold — watch for formal talks (exit signal).
actions.confidence_basis: Blockade = WAR_PREMIUM_RETURN confirmed. Gold is the primary beneficiary. P023 4★ on track. No reason to sell.

signal_details.M008: Gold war premium EXPANDING on Hormuz blockade. SETFGOLD at ₹130+. Hold through April 22.

actions.pnl_impact_if_right: SETFGOLD reaches ₹132-134 by April 17 — P023 RIGHT. Significant upside from ₹127.31.
actions.pnl_impact_if_wrong: If US-Iran deal announced suddenly → gold gives back gains quickly. Retain all holdings given current risk.
actions.risk: Surprise ceasefire breakthrough = gold pullback. Low probability given blockade escalation.

actions.instruction: IREDA — EARNINGS DAY. HIGH urgency. FY26 provisional data already strong: Loan book ₹93,075 Cr (22% growth, well above ₹70,000 Cr ADD trigger). Disbursements ₹34,946 Cr (+16% YoY). Sanctions ₹51,883 Cr (+9% YoY). Action triggers on Q4 earnings: (1) If Q4 net profit growth ≥25% YoY AND NPA stable/declining → ADD at ₹120-125 range. Target ₹132-135. (2) If Q4 results in-line with FY26 trend → HOLD, current price ₹123.35 is fair value. (3) If Q4 shows NPA deterioration or loan book guidance cut → AVOID adding. IREDA at ₹123.35 (April 13). FY26 loan book confirmation ₹93,075 Cr = strong fundamental case. Green energy tailwind: lower crude = government fiscal space for renewable capex.
actions.rationale_short: IREDA earnings April 16 — no action before then. Ceasefire = modest positive for capex environment.
actions.confidence_basis: Earnings April 16 is the catalyst. Ceasefire is a modest positive.
actions.risk: Earnings miss on loan book growth

actions.instruction: HOLD_FOR_RECOVERY — GRM thesis STRONGLY back on track. Brent at ~$93-96 vs $102.26 peak = SIGNIFICANT positive for gross refining margins. P017 target ₹200-210 (entry ₹182.70). MRPL at ~₹170.85 (April 13) — BELOW entry. Current scenario analysis: Brent $93-96 = wide GRM spread vs $102 peak. MRPL at ₹170.85 is undervalued if GRM recovery materializes in Q1FY27. Consider ADDING at ₹168-172 if Brent holds below ₹95 this week. Hard stop: ₹165 (if Brent spikes back above $100 on IRGC incident). Watch: MRPL Q4 results timing — any pre-results positioning opportunity.
actions.rationale_short: MRPL GRM thesis strongest ever — Brent $97.50 = GRM recovery runway widening. Hold.
actions.confidence_basis: UPGRADED to 4★ (April 10 midday). Brent at $91-93 = strong GRM recovery signal. MRPL at ₹182.70 with +10-15% to target ₹200-210. 28 days remaining. Talks neutral = no Brent spike risk.

signal_details.M001: Brent at $91-93 (April 10 midday) — GRM recovery thesis STRENGTHENED. Declined from $96.51 morning on Trump infrastructure strike delay.

actions.pnl_impact_if_right: MRPL recovers to ₹200-210 by May 8 — now higher confidence given Brent decline
actions.pnl_impact_if_wrong: Talks fail → Brent rebounds above $100 → GRM compression returns (now lower probability)
actions.risk: Islamabad talks failure → Brent rebounds → GRM thesis weakens. Probability now ~30% (down from 35%).

actions.instruction: WATCH — Iran talks accelerating = defense premium REDUCING. HIGH urgency on exit timing. P025 target ₹1,050-1,100 by April 22. ASTRA MICRO at ~₹1,019 (April 13). Risk: If Iran second round talks confirmed today (April 16) and market rallies, ASTRA MICRO may see 3-5% profit-booking as defense sector rotates out. ASTRA MICRO needs +3% to reach ₹1,050 target — possible but DECREASING probability. Action: If stock bounces to ₹1,040-1,050 on market rally → PARTIAL EXIT 30-40%. Hard stop: ₹980 (from P012). Below ₹1,000 = momentum turning. P025 grade likely WRONG if talks confirmed before April 22 — reassess April 17.
actions.rationale_short: Astra Micro surged to ₹1,132 on blockade news — P012 target exceeded. Raise profit trigger to ₹1,100-1,120. Consider 25-35% partial exit at ₹1,140+.
actions.confidence_basis: Defense premium at MAXIMUM in active blockade regime. Stock +11.9% from ₹1,012. P012 RIGHT, P025 tracking well ahead of schedule.

signal_details.S003: Defense electronics premium at MAXIMUM in blockade/standoff regime. M012 framework fully active.
signal_details.M012: Active US-Iran standoff = extended defense premium duration. Not a one-day event.

actions.pnl_impact_if_right: P012 RIGHT (₹1,050-1,080 exceeded). P025 tracking ahead of schedule. Position +100%+ from cost ₹565.
actions.pnl_impact_if_wrong: Surprise ceasefire → defense premium collapses → stock could fall 10-15%. Set stop at ₹1,080.
actions.risk: Surprise diplomatic breakthrough = defense premium unwind. Currently low probability given blockade.

market_actions.instruction: APRIL 15 UPDATE: Brent fell to $97.50 on April 14 (Indian holiday). Trump said Iran called and wants to negotiate — war premium EASING. Brent is now in the $95-100 WATCH ZONE. This is POSITIVE for MRPL (GRM recovery), INDIGO (ATF relief), NEGATIVE for SETFGOLD (reduce war premium). ASTRA MICRO: Diplomatic news = some defense premium compression. Watch zones: >$103 = ESCALATION RETURN (sell INDIGO, hold defense). $95-100 = CURRENT (WATCH ZONE — outreach sustained). $90-95 = FORMAL TALKS CONFIRMED (exit gold, buy airlines). <$90 = DEAL DONE.
market_actions.current_level: $97.50 (April 14 — fell from $102.26 on Iran outreach signal)
market_actions.midday_note: Trump delayed Iran infrastructure strikes to April 22 = immediate crude risk premium removed. Brent moved from 95-100 WATCH ZONE to 90-95 DE-ESCALATION CONFIRMED zone.

scenarios.above_100: REGIME CONCERN — war premium returning? Re-evaluate A002 (INDIGO) and A007 (MRPL GRM thesis)
scenarios.95_to_100: WATCH ZONE — Brent stabilization. MRPL thesis tracking. No action.
scenarios.90_to_95: CURRENT ZONE (April 10 midday) — De-escalation confirmed. MRPL recovery (P017) strong.
scenarios.below_88: DEMAND DESTRUCTION — good for airlines/autos but signals global recession risk

market_actions.instruction: HIGHEST PRIORITY EVENT: April 10 Islamabad talks (10:00 AM IST + 12:30 PM IST sessions). BOTH US AND IRAN CONFIRMED PARTICIPATION. Pakistan hosting. This is the NEXT regime-defining binary. Morning brief April 10 must lead with this outcome BEFORE all else. Three outcomes: (1) Talks progress = DE-ESCALATION_DEEPENING, ceasefire extends to 4+ weeks. (2) Talks neutral/delayed = DE-ESCALATION_REAL holds, 2-week ceasefire timeline intact. (3) Talks fail = WAR_PREMIUM_RETURN, re-activate A002 INDIGO sell trigger.
market_actions.current_level: Confirmed — Both parties attending. April 10 10:00 AM IST.

scenarios.talks_progress: BULLISH — Extend all positions. Accelerate SETFGOLD exit. Defense premium compresses. P019 ADANI PORT strengthens.
scenarios.talks_neutral: SIDEWAYS — Hold positions. 2-week ceasefire timeline intact. Review April 22 (ceasefire expiry).
scenarios.talks_fail: BEARISH — WAR_PREMIUM returns. Re-activate A002 (INDIGO SELL). Hold all SETFGOLD. ASTRA MICRO new high potential. Nifty -2-4%.

market_actions.instruction: TCS Q4FY26 results TODAY post-market (~3:40-4:20 PM IST). Board meeting convened. Analyst consensus: Revenue ₹69,932 cr (+8% YoY), PAT ₹13,919 cr (+14% YoY), EBIT margin ~25.3-25.4%. Deal TCV + FY27 guidance are the KEY watch items for P018 and P014 (Nifty IT outperform). Conference call 7:00 PM IST. P018 predicts TCS above ₹2,600 within 7 days — results today will largely determine direction. CHECK RESULTS TONIGHT AND SCORE P018 IN EOD WRAP.
market_actions.current_level: TCS at ₹2,555.30 (April 8 close). Results expected ~4 PM IST.

scenarios.beats_all_metrics: TCS above ₹2,650 → P018 RIGHT early → P014 confidence 4★ → Nifty IT gap-up April 10
scenarios.in_line: TCS stays ₹2,520-2,600 → P018 tracking → P014 intact → IT sector flat
scenarios.misses_guidance: TCS drops to ₹2,450-2,480 → P018 at risk → P014 confidence 2★ → IT sector gives back gains

action_summary.critical_action: APRIL 15 REGIME SHIFT: Iran outreach + Brent $97.50 changes urgency rankings. 1. MRPL (A007) — P017 GRM recovery now 5★, BEST POSITIONED stock for Brent decline. 2. INDIGO (A002) — Brent <$100 = ATF relief returning, HOLD firm. 3. ASTRA MICRO (A008) — Take 25-35% profit at ₹1,100+; set stop ₹1,080 (Iran diplomacy = defense premium risk). 4. SETFGOLD (A005) — Hold but watch for formal talks (exit trigger). 5. ADANI PORT (A004) — Upgraded on Hormuz re-open potential.
action_summary.highest_priority_today: 1. MRPL — Brent $97.50 = GRM thesis best ever. P017 5★. Hold all. 2. INDIGO — Brent <$100 = HOLD confirmed. Monitor Brent for $90-95 (strong buy). 3. ASTRA MICRO — Set hard stop at ₹1,080. Take partial profits at ₹1,100+. Watch Iran outreach. 4. SETFGOLD — Hold ₹130+. Iran outreach = prepare for partial exit trigger. 5. ADANI PORT — Upgraded thesis. Watch formal talks announcement.
action_summary.regime_change_summary: REGIME STABILIZED April 15: From BLOCKADE_ESCALATION to BLOCKADE_ESCALATION_STABILIZED. Trump said Iran called wanting negotiations on April 14. Brent fell $4.76 to $97.50 on the signal. No formal deal. Blockade in effect. April 22 is the next binary. Key implication: War premium IS easing but NOT gone. Positioning shifts from pure-defense to rotation toward oil-sensitive beneficiaries (MRPL, INDIGO).
